speaker: ISABELLA
text: And have you nuns no farther privileges?

speaker: NUN
text: Are not these large enough?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yes, truly. I speak not as desiring more,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But rather wishing a more strict restraint

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Upon the sisterhood, the votarists of Saint Clare.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Ho, peace be in this place!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Who’s that which calls?

speaker: NUN
text: It is a man’s voice. Gentle Isabella,

speaker: NUN
text: Turn you the key and know his business of him.

speaker: NUN
text: You may; I may not. You are yet unsworn.

speaker: NUN
text: When you have vowed, you must not speak with men

speaker: NUN
text: But in the presence of the Prioress.

speaker: NUN
text: Then, if you speak, you must not show your face;

speaker: NUN
text: Or if you show your face, you must not speak.

speaker: NUN
text: He calls again. I pray you answer him.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Peace and prosperity! Who is ’t that calls?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Hail, virgin, if you be, as those cheek-roses

speaker: LUCIO
text: Proclaim you are no less. Can you so stead me

speaker: LUCIO
text: As bring me to the sight of Isabella,

speaker: LUCIO
text: A novice of this place and the fair sister

speaker: LUCIO
text: To her unhappy brother, Claudio?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Why “her unhappy brother”? Let me ask,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The rather for I now must make you know

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am that Isabella, and his sister.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Gentle and fair, your brother kindly greets you.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Not to be weary with you, he’s in prison.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Woe me, for what?

speaker: LUCIO
text: For that which, if myself might be his judge,

speaker: LUCIO
text: He should receive his punishment in thanks:

speaker: LUCIO
text: He hath got his friend with child.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Sir, make me not your story.

speaker: LUCIO
text: ’Tis true.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I would not, though ’tis my familiar sin

speaker: LUCIO
text: With maids to seem the lapwing and to jest,

speaker: LUCIO
text: Tongue far from heart, play with all virgins so.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I hold you as a thing enskied and sainted,

speaker: LUCIO
text: By your renouncement an immortal spirit,

speaker: LUCIO
text: And to be talked with in sincerity

speaker: LUCIO
text: As with a saint.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: You do blaspheme the good in mocking me.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Do not believe it. Fewness and truth, ’tis thus:

speaker: LUCIO
text: Your brother and his lover have embraced;

speaker: LUCIO
text: As those that feed grow full, as blossoming time

speaker: LUCIO
text: That from the seedness the bare fallow brings

speaker: LUCIO
text: To teeming foison, even so her plenteous womb

speaker: LUCIO
text: Expresseth his full tilth and husbandry.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Someone with child by him? My cousin Juliet?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Is she your cousin?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Adoptedly, as schoolmaids change their names

speaker: ISABELLA
text: By vain though apt affection.

speaker: LUCIO
text: She it is.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, let him marry her!

speaker: LUCIO
text: This is the point.

speaker: LUCIO
text: The Duke is very strangely gone from hence;

speaker: LUCIO
text: Bore many gentlemen, myself being one,

speaker: LUCIO
text: In hand, and hope of action; but we do learn,

speaker: LUCIO
text: By those that know the very nerves of state,

speaker: LUCIO
text: His givings-out were of an infinite distance

speaker: LUCIO
text: From his true-meant design. Upon his place,

speaker: LUCIO
text: And with full line of his authority,

speaker: LUCIO
text: Governs Lord Angelo, a man whose blood

speaker: LUCIO
text: Is very snow-broth; one who never feels

speaker: LUCIO
text: The wanton stings and motions of the sense,

speaker: LUCIO
text: But doth rebate and blunt his natural edge

speaker: LUCIO
text: With profits of the mind: study and fast.

speaker: LUCIO
text: He—to give fear to use and liberty,

speaker: LUCIO
text: Which have for long run by the hideous law

speaker: LUCIO
text: As mice by lions—hath picked out an act

speaker: LUCIO
text: Under whose heavy sense your brother’s life

speaker: LUCIO
text: Falls into forfeit. He arrests him on it,

speaker: LUCIO
text: And follows close the rigor of the statute

speaker: LUCIO
text: To make him an example. All hope is gone

speaker: LUCIO
text: Unless you have the grace by your fair prayer

speaker: LUCIO
text: To soften Angelo. And that’s my pith of business

speaker: LUCIO
text: ’Twixt you and your poor brother.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Doth he so

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Seek his life?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Has censured him already,

speaker: LUCIO
text: And, as I hear, the Provost hath a warrant

speaker: LUCIO
text: For ’s execution.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Alas, what poor ability’s in me

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To do him good?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Assay the power you have.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: My power? Alas, I doubt—

speaker: LUCIO
text: Our doubts are traitors

speaker: LUCIO
text: And makes us lose the good we oft might win

speaker: LUCIO
text: By fearing to attempt. Go to Lord Angelo

speaker: LUCIO
text: And let him learn to know, when maidens sue

speaker: LUCIO
text: Men give like gods; but when they weep and kneel,

speaker: LUCIO
text: All their petitions are as freely theirs

speaker: LUCIO
text: As they themselves would owe them.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’ll see what I can do.

speaker: LUCIO
text: But speedily!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I will about it straight,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: No longer staying but to give the Mother

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Notice of my affair. I humbly thank you.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Commend me to my brother. Soon at night

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I’ll send him certain word of my success.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I take my leave of you.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Good sir, adieu.
